content.text: How can I automatically generate the folder structure and modules for a Python package based on an existing list of classes?

Since this function focuses purely on orchestration, it delegates the heavy lifting to a dedicated internal `PackageBuilder`. It takes your input classes and configurations to start the generation process.

```python
def to_python_package(classes, target_folder, parent_package=None, indent=DEFAULT_INDENT):
    '''
    This function can be used to build a python package representation of pyschema classes.
    One module is created per namespace in a package matching the namespace hierarchy.

    Args:
        classes: A collection of classes to build the package from
        target_folder: Root folder of the package
        parent_package: Prepended on all import statements in order to support absolute imports.
            parent_package is not used when building the package file structure
        indent: Indent level. Defaults to 4 spaces
    '''
    PackageBuilder(target_folder, parent_package, indent).from_classes_with_refs(classes)
```

This approach uses the built-in `PackageBuilder` class to handle complex logic regarding namespace mapping and file creation. The success of this function relies entirely on how that builder interprets the provided schema interfaces.
What happens if one of the defined classes has a dependency (another imported class) that is not present in the input list?

Typically, such tools enforce strict completeness, so if a reference cannot be resolved within the provided `classes` collection, the build process will likely fail with an unresolved dependency error.
Got it; I should ensure all my dependencies are correctly listed before running this function.
